sketches.mechanism: Ownership transfer (move semantics) — after giving a value to someone, the original binding can no longer use it
sketches.resonance: Giving yourself fully to another means you no longer possess what you gave; the compiler enforces that love is not duplicable.

sketches.mechanism: The `Nothing` type — a type with no instances that is the return type of functions that never return
sketches.resonance: A function that promises to return Nothing keeps its promise perfectly, because there is nothing it could ever give back.

sketches.mechanism: Method `missing` — when an object receives a message it doesn't understand, `method_missing` is invoked as a last resort
sketches.resonance: Every name you call me that I don't recognize, I hear as the same silence.
sketches.draft:
class You
  def method_missing(*)
    nil
  end
end

You.new.are_you_there?
You.new.do_you_remember?
You.new.please?
